node.title: Got rejected from 10 FAANG level companies! Every company i got interview for!

topic.title: Got rejected from 10 FAANG level companies! Every company i got interview for!

post.content:
I'm a junior cs student at a top tier University at India. My internship season started back in July 2022. At that time i wasn't completely prepared with DSA, I did two courses on DSA but hadn't practises problem solving much. I was familar with majority of questions but have practised them during April.  Companies came for SWE internship role for this summer. 

Resume when the season started:
- Research Intern at industrial Lab. Work on ML(Intern)
- SWE intern at Government of India in Full stack devlopement. (Intern)
- Data Intern on a Research Project (Intern)
- Operating System designed using assembly language and C (Project)
- Action Recogintion using Deep Learning (Project)
- Implemented A*, BFS, DFS and compared them in React (Project)
- Job Portal- Full Stack Web App (Project)

**Got interview at D.E. Shaw**. Cleared round 1 coz of great resume and work experience. In round 2, got a little cocky and wasn't able to answer dsa questions where they asked me lru cache and how to create a text editor. Rejected

**Got interview at Sprinklr**. Got lc hard, totally panicked. Wasn't able to think at all. Although my approach was in right direction i wasn't able to materialize on it. I judt wanted to leave. But they gave me lru cache and told me to implement it. Was doing it, but they said time is less, use c++ stl. I didn't know how to use the stl. Rejected

**Got interview at Uber**. Cleared round 1, was a lc medium graph question. As had solved that question previously was able to do it. In round 2, it was a maths based question..cp question. I wasn't able to give the optimal answer. (Based on mathematical observation) Rejected

**Got interview at Intuit**. Was asked 2 questions. Not able to solve the first one, was LC hard. Second one was LC easy..did it. Rejected

I had already solved lot of dsa questions before the season started but haven't practiced problem solving much. During this time i constantly solved striver sheet( comprises 180 interview questions). And now i was confident.

**Got interview at Morgan Stanley**. Asked to implement a decision tree coz that was on my projects. I wasn't able to. Gave around 8-10 back to back answers on DSA but didn't matter as i didn't implement dt. Rejected

**Got interview at Siemens**. I didn't want to apply, but only applied, as i didn't got a single interview for past 20 companies. I didn't want to get selected so deliberately answered wrong. Knew 90% of the answers. Rejected

This time i got selected for an international research internship. And the season ended. It's around December. Also cracked an international hackathon with top 10 rank.

I tried a lot of off campus opportunities but got very less opportunities, partially due to my fault as i applied less.

**Got interview at Trexquant**. Answered quant questions okayish. Wasn't able to think answer for ML question. Code assignment was very poor. Rejected

March starts, and i go hardcore in applying and get.

**Got interview at Coinbase**. The hr told me to write production code. For that i wrote a 100 line code. Solved first question. Explained second question, wrote 90% of the next question. Rejected. First was lc easy and other lc medium. The next question can be more than 2000+ on codeforces if the optimal answer was needed. He was okay with brute.

**Got interview at Microsoft Research for Research Internship**. Solved LC hard with brute optimal. Messed up as i by mistake wrote the wrong answer when explaining the brute. Got 2 question on my projects. He concluded i don't have the experience and fundamentals he needed. Rejected

**Got at interview at Applied Scientist Internship for Amazon**. Got lc medium with lot of optimisations that he needed. Did all. Then asked another question, he was quite happy with my performance already on first. But i solved the next question too. And wrote the code.
Got ML round with the hiring manager. The round was for 1 hour. But my performance was so bad he ended in 30 mins. Was asked 4 questions, based on my projects. Was only able to answer 2. Response awaited but i know, I'll be rejected.

I was rejected for all the companies I ever gave the interview for. I have my placement season coming, where companies come for new grad full time roles. Although i have improved my problem solving. It's not perfect as i was also packed with research, ML, applying to get interviews. I can now solve 2 questions in codeforces div 2 and 4 in codeforces div 3. At leetcode, i can solve starting three questions. I would be also leaving for abroad for my internship, so what should i focus on for cracking the interviews. Any advice would be appreciated.

Edit 1- Rejected from Amazon AS Intern. Got a 6 month cooldown too. :(

Edit 2- I got a Product Engineer FTE offer from Sprinklr! Cracked the first day zero company that visited my campus. Thanks a lot everyone for your kind words, they really boosted my morale and confidence. Check this [post](https://leetcode.com/discuss/interview-experience/3884540/From-Zero-Internship-Offer-to-cracking-Day-Zero-company-Sprinklr-as-a-Product-Engineer-(Full-Time)) out for detailed experience.

Edit 3- I also got quite few interview from some startups during my internship hunt. 

1. International **US Startup** that was opening their location in India. They were hiring for a **Data Analyst, role full time**. They liked my resume and got a call scheduled with their VP of Data Analysis. I really liked the guy, as he was VP at Goldman Sachs US location for 5 years and had really nice experience. The moment I joined the interview, he told me to mention all the other things I didn't mention on the resume. It was a great round, did really well, the VP was impressed. Now the weirdest thing, they were hiring me full-time and I was in college for 2 years more! They didn't even see that and scheduled the meeting. Nevertheless, I wasn't that interested in the role and gave the interview for practice. Although, I told them, I would be glad if they could offer an internship. I got a reply after 2 days, that they won't be able to offer the internship and want full-time engineers and would love to hire me in the future. 

2. Got an interview from a **Netherlands business working on Computer Vision and Deep Learning**. I was quite serious about my time commitment and mentioned the expected stipend in the first meeting. I told them, at least 1000 euros per month and they were quite shocked to hear that number. They highlighted how they don't even pay that much to people graduating from University of Amsterdam. For me, it was a reasonable offer, as I already had one international internship offer and that amount was considered good as per Top Indian Startups. They called me into one more meeting for negotiation but I told them I would be taking my other internship and would reach out in the future if I have available time for an internship or Full Time. 

3. Got a call scheduled from a **French Startup with a Billon Dollar Valuation**! This was really my dream startup. Senior Vice President forwarded my resume to Vice President. I got a call with him, told him what i can do, what i can't. I don't know, how it went, as i woke up 10 minutes before the call and didn't study much about my resume. It didn't go that bad and he was happy from my experience and expectations. The dates i mentioned were in such a manner that i could have gone to France, come back for my exams and go back for the internship. It was the perfect work and stipend! But, unfortunately, the VP mentioned they weren't able to find anyone to mentor me during the internship. Felt really bad, because really wanted to go to France and work with their amazing team. The founders were Nobel Laureates!
Hard luck mate. Congrats on your international internship offer

Appeared & Failed at least 30 interviews to make it to a product company -- my own experience. 
So, keep hustling and always remember one thing -- There are thousands of candidates who wish to see  interview invites in their mailbox. You're getting interview calls, which is the hardest part than interviews. 
You can't always perform best in every interview appearance. 
The key thing is: improve your "average" interview performance after every interview appearance -- by regular practice and mock ups.

3.9 YOE here, got rejected from 15+ companies in past around 3 months PayU, Razorpay, MPL, Reliace, Phonepe, Coinbase, Cars24, Spinny, BharatPe, Paypal to name a few. Refer or tell me more companies to apply to.

u did a great job tryign bro , many of us havent been doing a lot of dsa or havent been apllying for comapnies, u have been applying and giving all the interviews , u have been learning from all your mistakes whereas more than 99 % of people havent been doing anything, be proud of urself and great man u have interiewed for 10 faang level companies , takes great skill, u will get it mate all the best

Amidst other things, I see persistence and the improvement that comes with consistency. Keep going buddy ! You got this. You know what you need to work on by now such as: 
1. Know your Resume IN and OUT - you should be able to implement/elaborate on everything in your Resume
2. Keep practising Leetcode. With your experience of interviewing and practising, you're definitely going to be able to crack it. 

Best of luck !
Fortunately. I made through ML Round for Applied Scientist Internship at Amazon. All my ML Learnings helped me. Don\'t worry, Something big must be awaiting for you. Best of Luck!
Please mention the projects you\'ve developed ;
Curious, what do you think you gained by giving wrong answers deliberately? You can always reject an eventual offer if you don\'t want to join.
hi, can you explain trexQuant assignment\n
Hey Hii buddy For trexquant I am having an interview for alpha researcher role . Could you please provide any guidance? Thanks
Feels like it\'s my story, with many of my friends being placed at either FAANG or Big 4, it motivates yet demotivates at the same time. Guess what, let\'s just keep grinding :)
You got on inteview at DE Shaw and you\'re a Junior. With a bit more prep, you\'ll land a good offer eventually.
